project_type: TypeScript monorepo (pnpm + Turborepo) with 9 Next.js/Fastify apps and 28 shared packages
build_system: pnpm@10.28.1 + Turborepo. Each package has its own build/typecheck/test scripts. turbo.json defines task pipeline with build depending on ^build.
test_framework: Vitest 2 with globals: true, environment: node. Co-located __tests__/ dirs and .test.ts files.
verification_instructions: 1. pnpm turbo typecheck (all packages must pass). 2. pnpm turbo test (all tests pass). 3. find apps -path '*/api/*' -name '*.ts' | wc -l = 0. 4. grep -r 'new Map<' apps/ --include='*.ts' | grep -v 'test|spec|cache' = empty.
snapshot_or_generated_files: packages/database uses 'prisma generate' to produce @prisma/client. Run pnpm --filter @quant/database build to regenerate.
setup_instructions: pnpm install at repo root. No frozen-lockfile needed for adding new deps.
environment_constraints: COMMON_DEPENDENCIES network mode - can install from npm registry. No Docker daemon for testing. Prisma generate may fail without DB but typecheck still works with generated types.
contribution_requirements: Conventional commits. TypeScript strict mode, no any, no @ts-ignore. All validation uses Zod. crypto.randomBytes/randomUUID for security. Every new service gets co-located .test.ts.
key_patterns: Packages use @quant/<name> workspace imports. Backend apps use @quant/server-core createApp() for Fastify setup. Each app's backend/ has app.ts + routes/ + services/ + __tests__/. Existing BaseRepository in packages/database is minimal (just holds prisma ref). docker-compose.dev.yml has postgres, redis, minio, mailhog, meilisearch, nats.
directory_structure: Root: apps/ (9 apps each with api/, backend/, src/), packages/ (28 shared libs), services/ (empty service stubs with Dockerfiles only), docker-compose.dev.yml. Each app's api/ folder is legacy with in-memory Map storage. Each app's backend/ is the canonical Fastify server using Prisma.
